intro: Pedilonum subflavidum, also known as the Pedilantus, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can aid in wound healing and the treatment of various infections. The bioactive constituents of Pedilantus include alkaloids, phenolic compounds, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Pedilantus is often prepared into teas, infusions, and decoctions, and it is also used to make tinctures and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
